tasks.name: bringup IC-testbench firmware-setup and python
tasks.description:
[@Aswin Raj K](#user_mention#89291671) ported firmware support for the QS223 [here](https://github.com/Quasistatics/FW-APP-QS223-testbench/commits/testbench_setup/), and has left python support in progress [here](https://github.com/Quasistatics/IC-testbench/tree/testbench_setup). I plan on testing and completing his work, but want help with the initial python bringup on the test machine, along with validation of the serial ports.

This both helps with the bringup and exposes you to the system, giving more context for any support the IC-team needs during the day in Bangalore.

Steps AFAIU:
1. clone Aswins branch linked above, setup IC-testbench, run GUI via python on 223\_FPGA\_BLR (ask Ushasi for access if needed)
2. ensure both daqboards are interfaceable via usb, flashed with the right firmware, from the firmware branch linked above.
3. I don't know if there's any basic connection-related smoke-tests we do - presumably validating USB ports, and maybe sending data bidirectionally to the daqboard

tasks.name: N3: Top-Schematic closure with digital port connections

comments.text:
Hi @KiritkumarP:
Hi Sumon Bose, 
Please find top level connection path:
/home/ArchismanGhosh/CC/QS223_Archisman_model/QS223_TOP_SUMON_24thNOV
In general most of the connections are okay. But please do check. It is quite easy for me to miss as this is my 1st time and few signal naming is really confusing. Sometime timezone works against me in clarifying the signals. 
Some of the PS signals (specificallly status ones) are not super clear to me. please check and let me know. 
Sayantan Dey there is a really confusing naming pattern related to spi_addr<5:0> between mem_controller, fifo, and PS... There are signals like spi_addr, spi_addr_final, spui_addr_to_controller etc. Please double check those and let me know. I see similar things are followed from 222. Please clarify this over chat or with Sumon da once. 
Please let me know if anything else is missing. Sorry for the delay, I was a bit occupied in all other tasks as you know. 
 Please let me know if you have any question
It is done from my side
comments.author: Archisman Ghosh

tasks.blockerReason:
Delay Reason @pm:
1. Summary: Delay due to unclear signal naming conventions and missing clarification on PS signals.
2. Technical: Confusing naming patterns related to spi_addr<5:0> between mem_controller, fifo, and PS, requiring further investigation.

tasks.name: QS223 Wi-R bypass-mode test implementation & support
tasks.description:
The Wi-R peripheral in the QS223 supports two interface modes: through a Cortex-M0 core in the integrated circuit over APB, and in "bypass mode, " allowing (Q)SPI access.

*   A list of test cases, with links to existing applications is available [here](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/personal/suhaib_ishaque_ixana_ai/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B04296BD7-A4B8-46BE-8DB6-3B9A6E40BE80%7D&file=QS223_test_plan.xlsx&action=default&mobileredirect=true).
*   The current state is that unicast SPI firmware seems correct, and there are intermittent failures that seem hardware related (test setup, or RTL)
*   There are some docs available [here](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-63837/8cqezuv-77137) \- they aren't necessary at the current stage, as preliminary development is complete. This is the most helpful page, with [the mem\_to\_array.py](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-63837/8cqezuv-77397) for config updates, and test flow specifications more generally.

Objective:
*   support the IC team with firmware bugfixes and config updates while they execute tests in bypass mode - they will likely drive the order, the current test-in-progress is SPI, which is setup on 223\_FPGA\_BLR

tasks.name: QS223 CUSTOM LAYOUT

comments.text:
Kirit: Delay reason 
1) While designing the UWB filter, we had to do a couple of iterations to meet the bandwidth requirements. 
2) We changed the LPF layout a few times to reduce parasitics onthe  LPF o/p node. We had to bring RSSI and AGC i/p close to the LPF output to reduce the parasitic cap. 
3) UWB Clock buffer: We changed the clock buffer layout a few times to meet the delay requirements. 
4) We had to modify the integrator to meet the CDR PLL 320MHz requirements 
5) There was a change in BR_mixer and BR LPF design 
6) Added source follower at the output of ED and LPF 
7) Changed floor plan many times to meet the top-level routing requirements and optimize the critical signal path. (We brought BE_top closer to the ED o/p and SS_top closer to the LPF/ED o/p. 
8) There was a change in the Spectrum sensor design.
comments.author: KiritkumarP

comments.text: Testing automation comments are working with task @Avik Majumder
comments.author: Pushpal Das

tasks.blockerReason:
Delay Reason @pm:
1. Summary: The task was delayed due to multiple design iterations and changes to meet various requirements.
2. Technical: Design iterations for UWB filter, LPF layout, clock buffer, CDR PLL, BR_mixer, and Spectrum sensor designs contributed to the delay.

comments.text:
Kirit: Delay reason 
1) While designing the UWB filter, we had to do a couple of iterations to meet the bandwidth requirements. 
2) We changed the LPF layout a few times to reduce parasitics onthe  LPF o/p node. We had to bring RSSI and AGC i/p close to the LPF output to reduce the parasitic cap. 
3) UWB Clock buffer: We changed the clock buffer layout a few times to meet the delay requirements. 
4) We had to modify the integrator to meet the CDR PLL 320MHz requirements 
5) There was a change in BR_mixer and BR LPF design 
6) Added source follower at the output of ED and LPF 
7) Changed floor plan many times to meet the top-level routing requirements and optimize the critical signal path. (We brought BE_top closer to the ED o/p and SS_top closer to the LPF/ED o/p. 
8) There was a change in the Spectrum sensor design.
